title: Intensely Crispy Egyptian Flatbread
description: Savory Egyptian flatbread with a crispy texture, perfect for a lazy Sunday, made with strong white flour and cooked in a ttukbaegi earthenware pot, this dish is low in calories and friendly to various diets
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour, salt, sugar, and yeast.
Gradually add the warm water to the dry ingredients and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with a damp cloth, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Preheat the ttukbaegi earthenware pot over medium heat.
Punch down the dough and divide it into 4 equal pieces.
Roll out each piece into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
Brush the tops with olive oil and cook in the preheated pot for 2 minutes on each side, until crispy and golden brown.
